design_principles.two_handed_weapons: Higher damage but often have negative features
design_principles.ranged_weapons: Smaller damage dice compared to melee of same tier
design_principles.positive_features: Reduce damage die or bonus to balance
design_principles.negative_features: Increase damage die or bonus to compensate
design_principles.secondary_weapons: Lower damage bonuses, only one-handed, often support primary attacks

primary_weapon_features.name: Brutal
primary_weapon_features.description: When you roll the maximum value on a damage die, roll an additional damage die.
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Positive feature - may reduce base damage

primary_weapon_features.name: Burning
primary_weapon_features.description: When you roll a 6 on a damage die, the target must mark a Stress.
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Positive feature - may reduce base damage

primary_weapon_features.name: Cumbersome
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Negative feature - may increase base damage

primary_weapon_features.name: Deadly
primary_weapon_features.description: When you deal Severe damage, the target must mark an additional HP.
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Positive feature - may reduce base damage

primary_weapon_features.name: Dueling
primary_weapon_features.description: When there are no other creatures within Close range of the target, gain advantage on your attack roll against them.

primary_weapon_features.name: Heavy
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Negative feature - may increase base damage

primary_weapon_features.name: Massive
primary_weapon_features.description: −1 to Evasion; on a successful attack, roll an additional damage die and discard the lowest result.

primary_weapon_features.name: Powerful
primary_weapon_features.description: On a successful attack, roll an additional damage die and discard the lowest result.
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Positive feature - may reduce base damage

primary_weapon_features.name: Quick
primary_weapon_features.description: When you make an attack, you can mark a Stress to target another creature within range.
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Positive feature - may reduce base damage

primary_weapon_features.name: Reliable
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Strong positive feature - significant damage reduction

primary_weapon_features.name: Reloading
primary_weapon_features.description: After you make an attack, roll a d6. On a result of 1, you must mark a Stress to reload this weapon before you can fire it again.
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Negative feature - may increase base damage

primary_weapon_features.name: Scary
primary_weapon_features.description: On a successful attack, the target must mark a Stress.
primary_weapon_features.balance_note: Positive feature - may reduce base damage

secondary_weapon_features.name: Paired
secondary_weapon_features.description: +2 to primary weapon damage to targets within Melee range

tier_1_broadsword.name: Broadsword
tier_1_broadsword.design_note: Reliable feature reduced damage from d8+1 to d8+0

tier_2_greatsword.name: Greatsword

feature.name: Massive
feature.description: −1 to Evasion; on a successful attack, roll an additional damage die and discard the lowest result.

tier_2_greatsword.design_note: Standard two-handed Tier 2 damage with mixed feature

design_principles.high_protection_armor: Higher Armor Score and thresholds usually have negative features
design_principles.light_armor: Low protection for tier often has positive features
design_principles.balance_rule: Armor with both high slots AND high thresholds should almost never have beneficial features

features.name: Flexible
features.balance_note: Positive feature - typically on lighter armor

features.name: Very Heavy
features.balance_note: Strong negative feature - allows much higher protection

features.name: Resilient
features.description: Before you mark your last Armor Slot, roll a d6. On a result of 6, reduce the severity by one threshold without marking an Armor Slot.
features.balance_note: Positive feature - may reduce protection values

features.name: Fortified
features.description: When you mark an Armor Slot, you reduce the severity of an attack by two thresholds instead of one.
features.balance_note: Strong positive feature - significant protection reduction needed

tier_1_leather_armor.name: Leather Armor
tier_1_leather_armor.design_note: Low protection compensated by positive feature

tier_2_chainmail.name: Chainmail
tier_2_chainmail.design_note: High protection balanced by negative feature

design_principles: Should illustrate and support character motivations
Allow attachment to existing equipment via gems/modifications
Recipes encourage consumable use by making them renewable
Useful enough to keep but not so powerful players hoard them
Don't build encounters expecting PC consumable use

creation_guidelines.power_level: Less powerful than permanent features but more than standard actions
